steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The statement must be completed with the bear that has the lowest approximate weight in the table. Scan the weight column to find the minimum value, then read off that bear's sex and age.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — female that was 13 years old.: The 13-year-old female weighs 400 lbs — not the lowest. ✗
  • Choice B — female that was 6 years old.: The 6-year-old female weighs 800 lbs — the heaviest in the table, not the lowest. ✗
  • Choice C — male that was 10 years old.: The 10-year-old male weighs 375 lbs — lower than A and B, but not the minimum. ✗
  • Choice D — male that was 3 years old.: Bear 122, a 3-year-old male, weighs 200 lbs — the lowest value in the table. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
